subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear section headers (e.g. 2.1, 3.2) provide a strong organizational backbone
Wellâarticulated aim statement listing each investigative step, offering logical roadmap
Tables and figures are placed near relevant text, supporting navigation and coherence
weaknesses: Overly lengthy anecdotal introduction interrupts mathematical focus
Informal jokes and digressions reduce conciseness and relevance
Fullâpage images and occasional repetition detract from overall coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work at band 3 for coherence and organization but band 2 for conciseness. The exploration is coherent and logically developed, with clear structure, but informal digressions and repetitive passages prevent it reaching the top band. A score of 3 reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Clear tabulated data facilitates multiple representations of volume change
Effective use of tables, scatter plots with polynomial fit and error bars to support analysis
Generally understandable flow with most notation (e.g. W(x), Wâ²(x)) used correctly
weaknesses: Unit notation is inconsistent and axes on figures lack labels
Key steps (e.g. reversing Newtonâs law) are omitted, forcing reader to infer logic
Inconsistent rounding of coefficients and conflation of confidence intervals with error bars
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are gaps in derivations and notation consistency (band 2), the representations are used effectively (band 3) and the overall mathematical communication is good. Awarding 3 balances these aspects.

strengths: Investigation is clearly framed around the studentâs personal cooking challenge
Designing bespoke experiments (e.g. stoveâpower estimation) shows independent initiative
Informal quotes and personal narrative add genuine personality
weaknesses: Mathematical approach remains within familiar territory, limiting originality
Creative ideas are solid but not sufficiently novel for the highest band
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands scored at band 2: clear personal approach and evidence of independent thinking, but not at the level of outstanding innovation. A score of 2 accurately reflects this performance.

strengths: Meaningful commentary on experimental limitations and practical feasibility
Thoughtful analysis of errors and assumptions in models
Clear evaluation of outcomes with brief discussion of future improvements
weaknesses: Critical analysis lacks depth and rigour for higher bands
Future improvements and methodological critiques are only sketched superficially
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection strands sit firmly in band 2âmeaningful but not substantial critical reflection. The work shows thoughtful appraisal but does not reach the depth or insight required for band 3, so a score of 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Appropriate use of cubic regression and justification for model choice
Correct derivative work for identifying maxima and sound algebraic manipulations
Effective application of Q=mcÎT and logarithmic rearrangement to solve for time
Use of conductivity formula to estimate burn times demonstrates innovative application
weaknesses: Some broad physical assumptions (e.g. temperature ranges) reduce precision
Reversal of Newtonâs law without derivation and unit inconsistencies indicate minor gaps
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the mathematics is largely correct and applied innovatively (band 4), minor errors and unsupported derivations prevent the top band. A score of 4 recognises the effective and comprehensive use of relevant mathematics throughout.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are generally understandable, yet steps are sometimes skipped (e.g., no justification for reversing Newtonâs law), which forces the reader to infer missing logic.  Consequently the structure is only âsomewhatâ clear.

The student demonstrates correct derivative work, reasonable regression, and mostly accurate numerical calculations.  Nonetheless, reversing Newtonâs law without derivation and unit inconsistencies indicate gaps in understanding, so the work corresponds to âgood understanding with minor errorsâ rather than thorough, completely correct mastery.

The student employs tables, scatter plots with polynomial fit, error bars and a simple exponential graph, which together illustrate the data reasonably well.  These representations genuinely support the argument, so the use can be considered âeffectiveâ though not sophisticated enough for the top band.

The exploration follows a recognisable narrative (introduction â¸ data collection â¸ modelling â¸ recipe â¸ conclusion).  While the storyâtelling tone is informal, each mathematical section follows logically from the previous one, allowing the reader to follow the argument without major jumps.  However, digressions, jokes and long anecdotal passages interrupt the flow, so the coherence cannot be considered âexcellent.â

Designing and carrying out volume tests, estimating stove power and formulating a recipe display creativity and initiative above the minimum required, yet these ideas stay within familiar territory and are not sufficiently novel for the highest band.

Results are analysed through derivative calculations, variance, and comparison with hypotheses; sources of error and unrealistic assumptions are acknowledged.  While thoughtful, the analysis lacks depth and rigour needed for insightful critique.

The conclusion states how well each heat level met the desired texture and how the recipe could be generalised, providing a clear evaluation.  Future improvements are only briefly sketched, so the discussion does not reach a comprehensive level.

The student comments on experimental limitations (human whisking rate, heat losses) and reflects on the practical feasibility of the recipe, demonstrating âmeaningfulâ rather than superficial reflection.  Critical appraisal of mathematical modelling choices is limited, preventing a higher band.

Standard symbols ( W(x), Wâ²(x), Q ) are used and derivatives are notated correctly, but unit notation is inconsistent and some variables are re-defined casually in the text.  Hence only âsomeâ appropriate mathematical language is achieved.

The entire investigation is framed around the studentâs personal cooking challenge; methods are selected and adapted for that purpose, indicating a clear personal approach, though not an exceptionally innovative one.

Much of the writing is relevant to the aim, yet the narrative contains sizeable amounts of humour and personal anecdote that do not advance the mathematics.  This, together with repetitive explanations of basic formulas, prevents the work from being genuinely concise.

The mathematics used (cubic regression, derivative for maximum, exponential heating model, specific-heat calculations) is relevant to the stated aims and is applied appropriately; however, parts of the work employ physics formulas with little mathematical development, so the use is only âappropriateâ rather than âeffectiveâ or âcomprehensive.â

Choosing an everyday context and designing bespoke experiments shows noticeable individual thought beyond classroom examples, but the mathematics itself remains elementary, limiting evidence of outstanding originality.

Headings and sub-headings (e.g. 2.1, 3.2, 4.1) give the report a clear backbone and the reader can locate each part of the investigation quickly.  Tables and figures are placed near the relevant text.  Nevertheless, occasional repetition, misplaced images and an over-long introduction detract from a fully âexceptionalâ structure.

Calculus and exponential modelling are at standard AA SL level, which is commensurate but not stretching for HL.  Consequently the level can be judged âcommensurate with course levelâ (band 3) but not fully âcommensurate and well-appliedâ.

annotations.comment: The section header is clear and immediately communicates the explorationâs focus, aiding organization and navigation.
The fullâpage image dominates the first page and detracts from the academic presentation; consider resizing or relocating it to maintain focus.
Include a concise subtitle under the main title that outlines the aim and scope of the investigation to improve relevance.
The anecdotal introduction is engaging but overly lengthy and informal, interrupting coherence; trim the narrative to stay focused.
The informal quote adds personality and personal engagement but could be better integrated into the academic tone.
The aim statement clearly lists each investigative step, providing a logical roadmap for the reader.
Referring to âmy motherâs knowledgeâ as a source is unreliable; critically assess and justify source credibility.
Tabulated data of volume change is presented clearly, facilitating later analysis and multiple representations.
The whisk rate â5 whisks per secondâ is assumed without measurement validation; discuss how this rate was maintained.
Add explicit axis labels and units to all figures (e.g. time in seconds, volume in millilitres) to enhance clarity.
Choosing a cubic fit is justified by identical data points at 0 and 10 s, showing logical model selection.
The rounding of polynomial coefficients varies between full precision and four decimal places; maintain consistency.
Include intermediate steps when differentiating the cubic to Wâ²(x) for full mathematical communication.
Repeating the 30 s whisk five times and calculating uncertainty shows good appreciation of experimental error.
The concept of error margin is applied but the explanation conflates confidence intervals and error bars; clarify statistical basis.
Deriving mean, variance and standard deviation formulas explicitly shows sound mathematical understanding.
Complete the blank âData (2)â table with actual boiling times to support subsequent calculations.
Presenting boiling times and temperature ranges is relevant, but broad assumptions (e.g. 140â160 Â°C) reduce precision.
Using Q=mcÎT appropriately applies calorimetry to estimate energy transfer.
The mixtureâs specific heat derivation assumes perfect mixing without heat loss; reflect on limitations of that assumption.
Reversing Newtonâs law of cooling for heating without derivation weakens mathematical communication; show full steps.
Computing K for multiple stove levels demonstrates independent initiative in adapting theory to context.
Logarithmic rearrangement to solve for time is correct, showing solid use of algebraic manipulation.
Time units mix minutes and seconds inconsistently in calculations; standardize units for clarity and accuracy.
Thermal conductivity model for the folded omelette is ambitious; discuss how conduction vs convection assumptions may affect results.
Applying the conductivity formula to estimate burn times shows comprehensive use of mathematics in a novel context.
Final recipe instructions are detailed and personal but lack explicit linkage to the derived mathematical results.
The conclusion reflects on personal learning experiences and limitations, demonstrating meaningful reflection.
The conclusion resumes anecdotal reflections but misses a concise summary of the key quantitative findings.
The bibliography includes relevant sources but citation formats are inconsistent; adopt one style throughout.
